config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diutamakan tanpa pembatasan, mudah sekali disalahgunakan oleh negara atau mayoritas untuk menekan orang yang lemah. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dijaga dengan baik.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily "the greater good" can become an excuse for trampling civil liberties. Public safety and shared responsibilities matter, but they have to be balanced against constitutional rights, due process, and protections for minorities who can be crushed by majority rule. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, the result is often abuse by government or social pressure rather than a healthier community. To me, a strong society is one that protects people first and asks them to contribute through fair, limited obligations.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pemaksaan, pembungkaman, atau pengabaian terhadap orang kecil, padahal justru hak individu itulah yang melindungi warga dari kesewenang-wenangan mayoritas dan negara. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, transparan, dan tetap menghormati martabat serta kebebasan tiap orang. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling sering mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu menyeimbangkan kepentingan bersama tanpa menginjak hak dasar warganya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should automatically outrank individual rights, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to justify overreach by government and the majority. That said, I also do not think individual rights are absolute in every situation; public health, safety, and basic order sometimes require real limits, but those limits should be narrow, transparent, and legally accountable. When society is given priority by default, the people who usually pay the price are minorities, dissidents, and anyone without power. To me, a legitimate society earns cooperation by protecting rights first and only restricting them when there is a clear, necessary, and fair reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis lebih tinggi daripada hak individu. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil, jadi saya justru melihat hak individu sebagai pagar utama agar negara dan mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Namun, saya juga tidak menganggap hak individu itu mutlak tanpa batas; dalam keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik dan ketertiban, pembatasan bisa sah asalkan jelas, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang baik bukan yang paling berani mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil mengejar kepentingan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the individual by default, because in the United States that kind of thinking has repeatedly been used to excuse government overreach and majority pressure. I also do not see individual rights as unlimited: quarantine, emergency rules, and other narrow restrictions can be justified when they are necessary, proportional, and subject to real oversight. What I reject is the idea that the collective should win automatically, since that usually ends up hurting minorities and unpopular people first. A healthy society protects rights as the starting point and then proves, case by case, why any limit is truly needed.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
